MRILib.BotValues.description: A utility class for storing and defining various robot-specific motor directions, wheel constants, and calculated values. It centralizes configuration parameters such as motor directions, wheel diameter, gear ratios, and tick counts per revolution, along with zero power behaviors for motors. This class is essential for consistent and configurable robot behavior.

A_TeleOp.description: TeleOp mode for controlling the ArmBotFF robot, featuring field-centric driving with joystick inputs and trigger-based speed control. It also integrates an ArmFSM for managing the robot's arm state and provides telemetry for robot position, heading, and arm state.

MRILib.opmodes.AutonExample.description: This class represents an autonomous robot program for a FTC competition. It utilizes a 'Bot' object for hardware interaction, a 'PIDController' for precise movement, and a 'DriveFSM' (Drive State Machine) to manage autonomous routines. The program defines target positions for a 'bucket' and executes a sequence of movements and actions, including moving the arm to specific states (e.g., 'ARM_UP', 'DROP', 'ARM_PICKUP'), picking up samples, and dropping them. It also includes commented-out code for AprilTag localization and telemetry updates for driver feedback.

MRILib.opmodes.ServoZero.description: TeleOp OpMode for controlling multiple servos on a robot. Allows selection of individual servos using the D-pad and adjustment of their positions using the 'A' and 'Y' buttons. Servo positions are normalized between 0 and 1. Trigger buttons activate servo movements, with the left trigger moving all servos and the right trigger moving the currently selected servo. Telemetry displays the current target position for each servo, indicating the selected servo with a '>'.

MRILib.util.Bpad.description: Represents a gamepad's state, including analog stick positions, trigger values, and button states. It tracks the current and previous states of various inputs to detect changes, such as button presses and stick movements, and provides a mechanism to query the state of individual buttons.

MRILib.util.Mathf.description: Provides utility methods for mathematical operations, specifically focusing on angle manipulation. It includes constants like TAU (2 * PI) and methods for wrapping angles to a standard range.

MRILib.statemachine.BotState.description: Represents an abstract state within a Finite State Machine (FSM). It defines the basic structure for states, including a name for identification and lifecycle methods (start, update, end) that can be overridden by concrete subclasses to implement state-specific behavior. The class also provides default implementations for these methods and a toString method that returns the state's name.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmFSM.description: Manages the state machine for a robot arm, handling autonomous and tele-operated modes. It orchestrates arm movements and actions through distinct states, allowing for complex behaviors to be defined and executed sequentially or conditionally. The FSM supports transitions between states, one-time conditional actions, and integration with robot hardware via the ArmBotEasy interface.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmState.description: Represents a state within an Arm Finite State Machine (FSM). It manages transitions to other states based on defined conditions and provides static methods for managing and retrieving state instances. It extends BotState, inheriting basic state functionality.

MRILib.statemachine.Conditional.description: Holds a condition and a command, allowing for the execution of the command when the condition is met. It acts as a container for reactive logic.

MRILib.statemachine.DriveFSM.description: Manages a sequence of robot movement and action states, allowing for autonomous routines. It orchestrates transitions between states, executes state-specific logic (start, update, end), and handles movement commands with optional parallel actions and timeouts. The FSM maintains the robot's current position and heading, and can hold a specific pose.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy.description: Represents an arm subsystem for a robot, controlling slide and pivot motors, along with claw servos. It extends a base 'Bot' class, likely handling drivetrain and other common robot functionalities. This class provides methods for initializing, controlling, and querying the state of the arm and claw mechanisms.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF.description: Represents an arm-based robot with advanced control, including feedforward mechanisms for precise movement. It manages linear slide and pivot motors, along with multiple servos for claw manipulation. The class utilizes a dedicated thread for its PivotArmController to handle complex arm movements concurrently, ensuring smooth and responsive operation. It also tracks encoder positions and velocities for both arm components and provides methods for mode setting, encoder resetting, and safe thread termination.

MRILib.managers.RunnableStoppable.description: An interface that extends Runnable and adds methods for stopping the execution and updating the current state (position and velocity). This interface is suitable for tasks that require both continuous operation and the ability to be halted and have their internal state modified.

MRILib.managers.Bot.description: Represents the robot's chassis and drivetrain, encapsulating motors, odometry, IMU, and voltage sensing. It provides methods for initialization, state updates, and controlling robot movement using both robot-centric and field-centric drive commands. This class acts as a central hub for all drivetrain-related hardware and odometry data.

MRILib.managers.PivotArmController.description: Controls the pivot arm of a robot, managing its position, velocity, and power using a combination of feedforward and feedback control. It supports different operational modes like holding position, running to a target, or running with direct power input. The controller also calculates and compensates for gravitational forces acting on the arm.

MRILib.motion.HeadingPID.description: A PID controller specifically designed for angular or heading control. It extends the base PID class and modifies the update method to correctly calculate angular error using Mathf.angleWrap, ensuring the shortest angular distance is used. It also incorporates standard PID components like integral anti-windup and derivative calculation, adapted for angular inputs.

MRILib.motion.PID.description: Implements a Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) controller. This class calculates a control output based on the error between a target value and a measured current value. It includes features for integral anti-windup and derivative calculation. The controller maintains internal state for accumulated error, last error, and timestamps to compute the control signal over time. It is designed for use in control systems where continuous feedback and adjustment are required.

MRILib.motion.PIDController.description: Manages PID controllers for a robot's field-centric movement, including X, Y translation and rotational (theta) control. It interfaces with a Bot object for hardware access (odometry, motors) and Telemetry for debugging. The controller allows dynamic adjustment of maximum translational and rotational speeds and provides methods to set target positions and update movement commands based on current robot pose.

A_TeleOp#runOpMode().description: Initializes the robot hardware, including the ArmBotFF and ArmFSM. Configures the motors for running without encoders and enables brake mode. Sets up the ArmFSM with the robot instance and gamepad inputs. This method is called once at the beginning of the OpMode.

A_TeleOp#init().description: Executes the main teleoperation loop. Initializes the robot and ArmFSM, then starts multi-threaded operations for the bot. Inside the loop, it continuously updates the bot and arm state machine, processes gamepad inputs for driving (field-centric XYW control with speed scaling via left trigger) and arm state changes (setting to 'IDLE' on 'X' button press). It also resets the robot's heading if the 'start' button is pressed or the IMU yaw if 'dpad_up' is pressed. Telemetry data including runtime, position (X, Y, Heading), and the current arm state is displayed. The loop continues as long as the OpMode is active, and upon termination, it stops the bot's multi-threaded operations.

MRILib.opmodes.AutonExample#runOpMode().description: Initializes the robot hardware, sets up PID controllers for movement, and defines the autonomous routine. The routine includes moving to a starting position, interacting with a 'bucket' for sample collection and dropping, and executing timed sequences. It continuously updates the robot's state, movement, and arm status while the op mode is active, and displays telemetry data including target and current positions, and heading. Includes commented-out AprilTag localization logic.

MRILib.opmodes.ServoZero#init().description: Initializes the OpMode by mapping servo hardware components (elbow, wrist, roll, claw) and creating arrays to store servo objects and their target positions. The position list is initialized to a size of 4, corresponding to the four servos.

MRILib.opmodes.ServoZero#loop().description: Continuously runs during the OpMode. It handles servo selection using the D-pad (up/down) and adjusts the target position of the selected servo using the 'A' (increment) and 'Y' (decrement) buttons. Servo positions are wrapped around between 0 and 1. The left trigger moves all servos to their target positions, while the right trigger moves only the selected servo. Telemetry data is updated to display the current target position for each servo and indicate the currently selected servo.

MRILib.util.Bpad#get(String).description: Retrieves the boolean state of a specified button. Returns true if the button is currently pressed or has been pressed since the last update, and false otherwise. If the button state is not tracked, it defaults to false.

MRILib.util.Bpad#update(Gamepad).description: Updates the gamepad's state based on the input from a Gamepad object. It copies analog stick and trigger values, and tracks the current and previous states of all buttons and directional inputs to detect changes. Specifically, it records the current state of each button/input, its previous state, and a 'db' (digital button) state indicating a press that occurred in the current update cycle but not the previous one.

MRILib.util.Mathf#angleWrap(double).description: Normalizes a given radian angle to be within the range of -PI to PI. It first applies the modulo operator with TAU to bring the angle within a 0 to TAU range, then adjusts it to ensure it falls between -PI and PI.

MRILib.statemachine.BotState#BotState(St.description: Initializes a new BotState instance with a specified name.

MRILib.statemachine.BotState#BotState().description: Initializes a new BotState instance with the default name 'DEFAULT'.

MRILib.statemachine.BotState#start().description: Executes initialization logic when the state becomes active in the FSM. Default implementation does nothing.

MRILib.statemachine.BotState#update().description: Executes the main logic of the state repeatedly while it is active in the FSM, including checking for transition conditions. Default implementation does nothing.

MRILib.statemachine.BotState#end().description: Executes cleanup logic when the state is about to be deactivated before transitioning to another state. Default implementation does nothing.

MRILib.statemachine.BotState#toString().description: Returns the name of the state, used for logging and debugging.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmFSM#ArmFSM(ArmBot.description: Initializes the ArmFSM for autonomous operation. Sets the autonomous flag to true, assigns the provided ArmBotEasy instance, and calls the internal initialization method. It then sets the initial state to 'DEFAULT'.

Initializes the ArmFSM for tele-operated control. Sets the autonomous flag to false, assigns the provided ArmBotEasy instance, and stores references to the two Gamepad objects. It then calls the internal initialization method and sets the initial state to 'DEFAULT'.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmFSM#start().description: Executes the 'start' logic of the currently active arm state. This method is intended to be called once when a state is first entered.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmFSM#update().description: Executes the main update loop for the FSM. It first checks and executes any one-time conditional actions and then calls the 'update' logic of the current arm state.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmFSM#end().description: Executes the 'end' logic of the currently active arm state. This method is intended to be called once when a state is exited, typically before transitioning to another state.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmFSM#setState(Stri.description: Sets the current state of the FSM to a new state identified by its name. It retrieves the ArmState object using the provided name and then calls the overloaded setState method.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmFSM#setState(ArmS.description: Transitions the FSM to a new specified ArmState. If the new state is different from the current state, it calls the 'end' method of the current state, updates the current state, and then calls the 'start' method of the new state.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmFSM#transition().description: Evaluates the transition conditions defined within the current arm state. It iterates through the state's transitions and calls setState to transition to the first state whose condition evaluates to true.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmFSM#addConditiona.description: Adds a one-time conditional action to a list. This action consists of a condition (a Supplier that returns a boolean) and a command (a Runnable). The command will be executed once when the condition first becomes true.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmFSM#checkConditio.description: Iterates through a list of conditional actions, checking if their conditions are met. If a condition is true, it executes the associated command and removes the conditional from the list to prevent re-execution. Handles potential exceptions during condition evaluation or command execution.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmFSM#init().description: Initializes all the necessary ArmState objects for the FSM. New ArmState instances are created and automatically registered. This method defines the states, their start, update, and end logic, and their initial transition conditions.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmState#getState(St.description: Retrieves a previously initialized ArmState by its name from a static map. If the specified state is not found, it returns the state registered as the "DEFAULT" fallback.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmState#clearSavedS.description: Clears all statically stored ArmState instances from the internal HashMap, effectively resetting the state management.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmState#ArmState().description: Initializes a new ArmState with the name "DEFAULT" and registers it in the static states map.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmState#ArmState(St.description: Initializes a new ArmState with a specified name and registers it in the static states map.
Initializes a new ArmState with a specified name, optionally registering it in the static states map based on the 'save' flag.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmState#setDefault(.description: Sets an existing state, identified by 'stateName', as the new default fallback state. If 'stateName' does not correspond to an existing state, the default remains unchanged.

MRILib.statemachine.ArmState#setTransiti.description: Defines or updates a transition condition from the current state to a target state. The 'condition' parameter determines whether the transition should occur (true) or not (false).

MRILib.statemachine.ArmState#getTransiti.description: Returns the HashMap containing all defined transition conditions for this state, mapping target state names to their boolean evaluation results.

MRILib.statemachine.Conditional#Conditio.description: Initializes a new Conditional object with the specified condition and command. The condition is a Supplier that returns a boolean, and the command is a Runnable to be executed.

MRILib.statemachine.DriveFSM#DriveFSM(Bo.description: Initializes the DriveFSM with the robot instance and a PID controller for movement.

MRILib.statemachine.DriveFSM#start().description: Executes the initialization logic for the current state in the sequence.

MRILib.statemachine.DriveFSM#update().description: Executes the continuous update logic for the current state, typically called every loop.

MRILib.statemachine.DriveFSM#end().description: Executes the termination logic for the current state, called before transitioning to the next state.

MRILib.statemachine.DriveFSM#nextState().description: Transitions the FSM to the next state in the sequence by ending the current state, incrementing the state index, and starting the new state. It prevents transitioning past the last state.

MRILib.statemachine.DriveFSM#moveTo(doub.description: Adds a new state to the sequence that moves the robot to a target pose (x, y, theta). Optionally executes a parallel command and enforces a timeout. This method also updates the PID controller's target and stores the current pose as the last known position for holding.
Adds a moveTo state with a specified parallel command and a default timeout of 5 seconds.
Adds a moveTo state with a specified timeout and no parallel command.
Adds a moveTo state with a default timeout of 5 seconds and no parallel command.

MRILib.statemachine.DriveFSM#moveTo(Pose.description: Adds a moveTo state using a Pose2D object with default timeout and no parallel command.

MRILib.statemachine.DriveFSM#waitForSeco.description: Adds a new state to the sequence that waits for a specified duration while maintaining the last known position using the PID controller. Optionally executes a parallel command during the wait.
Adds a waitForSeconds state with a specified duration and no parallel command.

MRILib.statemachine.DriveFSM#run(Runnabl.description: Adds a state that immediately executes a given command without any waiting period, effectively running a command in parallel with the current state.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#ArmBotEasy(Ha.description: Constructs an ArmBotEasy instance, initializing base bot functionalities and then specifically initializing the arm's motors and servos using the provided HardwareMap.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#initMotors(Ha.description: Initializes the slide and pivot DC motors by retrieving them from the HardwareMap, resetting their encoders, setting their directions, and configuring them to run using encoder feedback.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#initServos(Ha.description: Initializes the claw, claw pitch, and claw roll servos by retrieving them from the HardwareMap.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#setArmMode(Dc.description: Sets the run mode for both the slide and pivot motors simultaneously to the specified DcMotor.RunMode.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#resetEncoders.description: Resets the encoders for the arm's slide and pivot motors to zero, preserving their current run modes, and then updates the stored encoder positions.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#updateEncoder.description: Updates the stored current and previous encoder positions for the slide and pivot motors, and also calls the superclass method to update drive motor encoders.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#getSlideMotor.description: Returns the DcMotorEx instance for the slide.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#getPivotMotor.description: Returns the DcMotorEx instance for the pivot.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#getSlidePos().description: Returns the last recorded encoder position of the slide motor. Ensure updateEncoders() is called prior for the most current value.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#getPivotPos().description: Returns the last recorded encoder position of the pivot motor. Ensure updateEncoders() is called prior for the most current value.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#getClawPitch(.description: Returns the Servo instance for the claw pitch mechanism.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#getClawRoll().description: Returns the Servo instance for the claw roll mechanism.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#getClaw().description: Returns the Servo instance for the main claw grip.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#getClawPitchP.description: Retrieves and returns the current commanded position of the claw pitch servo.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#getClawRollPo.description: Retrieves and returns the current commanded position of the claw roll servo.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#getClawPos().description: Retrieves and returns the current commanded position of the main claw grip servo.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#setClawPitch(.description: Sets the target position for the claw pitch servo to the specified value.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#setClawRoll(d.description: Sets the target position for the claw roll servo to the specified value.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#setClaw(doubl.description: Sets the target position for the main claw grip servo to the specified value.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#setPivot(int).description: Configures the pivot motor to move to a specific encoder target position by setting the target position, switching the run mode to RUN_TO_POSITION, and applying a default velocity.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#setSlides(int.description: Configures the slide motor to move to a specific encoder target position by setting the target position, switching the run mode to RUN_TO_POSITION, and applying a default velocity.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotEasy#setArm(int,in.description: Sets both the pivot and slide motors to move to their respective target encoder positions simultaneously by calling setPivot and setSlides.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#ArmBotFF(Hardwa.description: Initializes the ArmBotFF, setting up motors, servos, and starting the PivotArmController in a separate thread for concurrent operation. It calls parent constructor and initializes hardware components.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#initMotors(Hard.description: Initializes the slide and pivot DC motors by retrieving them from the HardwareMap, resetting their encoders, setting their direction and run modes, and configuring their zero power behavior to BRAKE. It also resets internal position tracking variables and updates current encoder positions.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#initServos(Hard.description: Initializes the claw pitch, roll, and grip servos by retrieving them from the HardwareMap. It does not set initial positions, but prepares them for use.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#setArmMode(DcMo.description: Sets the RunMode for both the slide and pivot motors to the specified mode, allowing for different control strategies like RUN_TO_POSITION or RUN_WITHOUT_ENCODER.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#resetEncoders().description: Resets encoders for both drive train (via superclass) and arm motors (slide and pivot) to zero. It preserves the current RunMode of the arm motors and resets internal position tracking variables.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#resetPrevEncode.description: Resets the internal tracking variables for the previous encoder positions of the slide and pivot motors to zero. It also calls the superclass method to reset drive train previous positions.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#updateControlle.description: Updates the PivotArmController with the current pivot motor's encoder position and velocity. This method should be called in the main OpMode loop after encoder positions have been updated.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#updateEncoders(.description: Updates the internal tracking of current and previous encoder positions for both drive train (via superclass) and arm motors (slide and pivot). It reads the current encoder positions and stores them, while shifting the previous positions to hold the last known values.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#getSlideMotor().description: Returns the DcMotorEx instance for the slide.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#getPivotMotor().description: Returns the DcMotorEx instance for the pivot.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#getSlidePos().description: Returns the current encoder position of the slide motor.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#getPivotPos().description: Returns the current encoder position of the pivot motor.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#getSlideVelocit.description: Returns the current velocity of the slide motor in encoder ticks per second.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#getPivotVelocit.description: Returns the current velocity of the pivot motor in encoder ticks per second.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#getClawPitch().description: Returns the Servo instance controlling the claw's pitch.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#getClawRoll().description: Returns the Servo instance controlling the claw's roll.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#getClaw().description: Returns the Servo instance controlling the claw's grip.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#getClawPitchPos.description: Returns the current position of the claw pitch servo, ranging from 0.0 to 1.0.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#getClawRollPos(.description: Returns the current position of the claw roll servo, ranging from 0.0 to 1.0.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#getClawPos().description: Returns the current position of the claw grip servo, ranging from 0.0 to 1.0.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#setClawPitch(do.description: Sets the target position for the claw pitch servo, clamping the input value between 0.0 and 1.0.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#setClawRoll(dou.description: Sets the target position for the claw roll servo, clamping the input value between 0.0 and 1.0.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#setClaw(double).description: Sets the target position for the claw grip servo, clamping the input value between 0.0 and 1.0.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#setPivot(int).description: Sets the pivot motor to run to a specific encoder target position by switching its mode to RUN_TO_POSITION and setting a predefined velocity.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#setPivotPower(d.description: Sets the pivot motor power directly by switching its mode to RUN_WITHOUT_ENCODER and applying the specified power level.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#setSlides(int).description: Sets the slide motor to run to a specific encoder target position by switching its mode to RUN_TO_POSITION and setting a predefined velocity.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#setArm(int,int).description: Sets both the pivot and slide motors to run to their respective target encoder positions simultaneously by calling individual setter methods.

MRILib.managers.ArmBotFF#stopMultiThread.description: Safely stops the background PivotArmController thread by signaling it to stop and then joining the thread with a short timeout. It includes error handling for interruptions and logs a warning if the thread does not terminate cleanly.

MRILib.managers.RunnableStoppable#stop().description: Stops the execution of the Runnable task. This method is intended to signal the task to cease its operations gracefully.

MRILib.managers.RunnableStoppable#update.description: Updates the internal state of the object with the provided current position and velocity.

MRILib.managers.Bot#Bot(HardwareMap).description: Constructs a Bot instance, initializing drive motors, IMU, odometry, and voltage sensor using default hardware map names. It also performs an initial odometry update and sets the current and last known positions.

MRILib.managers.Bot#initMotors(HardwareM.description: Initializes the drive motors using default hardware map names ('frontLeft', 'frontRight', 'backLeft', 'backRight') by calling the overloaded version.
Initializes the drive motors with specified hardware map names and sets their directions based on BotValues constants. It also resets and updates the initial encoder positions.

MRILib.managers.Bot#initIMU(HardwareMap).description: Initializes the Control Hub's IMU using the default hardware map name ('imu') by calling the overloaded version.

MRILib.managers.Bot#initIMU(HardwareMap,.description: Initializes the Control Hub's IMU with a specified hardware map name and configures its orientation based on BotValues constants (LOGO_DIR, USB_DIR). It then resets the IMU's yaw to zero.

MRILib.managers.Bot#initOdo(HardwareMap).description: Initializes the GoBilda Pinpoint Odometry Computer using the default hardware map name ('odo') by calling the overloaded version.

MRILib.managers.Bot#initOdo(HardwareMap,.description: Initializes the GoBilda Pinpoint Odometry Computer with a specified hardware map name, setting offsets, encoder resolution, and directions. It then resets the odometry's position and IMU reference.

MRILib.managers.Bot#update().description: Updates the robot's state by reading new data from the odometry device, updating the current and last known positions, and reading the latest drive motor encoder values.

MRILib.managers.Bot#updateEncoders().description: Updates the stored encoder positions for the drive train motors by saving the previous tick values and reading the current values from each motor.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getFL().description: Returns the front left drive motor instance.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getFR().description: Returns the front right drive motor instance.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getBL().description: Returns the back left drive motor instance.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getBR().description: Returns the back right drive motor instance.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getFLPos().description: Returns the last read encoder position of the front left motor. Ensure updateEncoders() has been called for the latest value.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getFRPos().description: Returns the last read encoder position of the front right motor. Ensure updateEncoders() has been called for the latest value.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getBLPos().description: Returns the last read encoder position of the back left motor. Ensure updateEncoders() has been called for the latest value.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getBRPos().description: Returns the last read encoder position of the back right motor. Ensure updateEncoders() has been called for the latest value.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getFLPosPrev().description: Returns the encoder position of the front left motor from the previous updateEncoders() call.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getFRPosPrev().description: Returns the encoder position of the front right motor from the previous updateEncoders() call.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getBLPosPrev().description: Returns the encoder position of the back left motor from the previous updateEncoders() call.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getBRPosPrev().description: Returns the encoder position of the back right motor from the previous updateEncoders() call.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getIMUHeading().description: Gets the robot's current heading (yaw) from the IMU, negated to likely align with standard FTC coordinate systems (counter-clockwise positive).

MRILib.managers.Bot#resetIMUHeading().description: Resets the IMU's yaw angle to zero based on the current orientation.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getPosition().description: Gets the robot's current position and heading as estimated by the odometry system, updated during the last call to update().

MRILib.managers.Bot#getLastPosition().description: Gets the robot's position and heading from the previous update cycle, useful for calculating displacement or velocity.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getOdoPosition().description: Gets the raw position directly from the GoBilda Pinpoint driver. This is a private method to encourage the use of the synchronized getPosition().

MRILib.managers.Bot#getX().description: Gets the current estimated X-coordinate of the robot in inches, as reported by the odometry system.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getY().description: Gets the current estimated Y-coordinate of the robot in inches, as reported by the odometry system.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getHeading().description: Gets the current estimated heading of the robot in degrees, as reported by the odometry system.

MRILib.managers.Bot#getVoltage().description: Gets the current battery voltage reported by the first available voltage sensor.

MRILib.managers.Bot#setPosition(double,d.description: Sets the robot's current position and heading in the odometry system using x, y coordinates and a heading in degrees. It converts the heading to radians and calls the Pose2D version.

MRILib.managers.Bot#setPosition(Pose2D).description: Overrides the current position tracked by the GoBilda Pinpoint Odometry Computer with a new Pose2D. It then immediately updates the odometry's internal state and the Bot's synchronized position variables.

MRILib.managers.Bot#setDirections().description: Sets the direction of all four drive motors based on the constants LEFTDIR and RIGHTDIR defined in BotValues, ensuring consistent forward motion.

MRILib.managers.Bot#setMode(DcMotor.RunM.description: Sets the DcMotor.RunMode for all four drive motors simultaneously to the specified mode.

MRILib.managers.Bot#resetEncoders().description: Resets the drive motor encoders to 0 without changing their current RunMode. It also resets the stored previous encoder tick values and updates the current values.

MRILib.managers.Bot#resetPrevEncoders().description: Resets the stored previous encoder tick values for all drive motors to zero.

MRILib.managers.Bot#enableBrakeMode(bool.description: Enables or disables the 'brake' zero power behavior for all drive motors. When enabled (true), motors resist movement; when disabled (false), they coast.

MRILib.managers.Bot#driveXYW(double,doub.description: Drives the robot using robot-centric Cartesian coordinates (rx, ry) and rotational velocity (rw). It calculates the required power for each Mecanum wheel, normalizes the powers to stay within [-1, 1], and compensates for battery voltage.

MRILib.managers.Bot#driveFieldXYW(double.description: Drives the robot using field-centric coordinates (fx, fy) for movement and robot-centric rotation (fw). It rotates the field-relative movement vector based on the robot's current heading (obtained from the IMU) and then calls the robot-centric drive method (driveXYW).

MRILib.managers.PivotArmController#Pivot.description: Initializes the PivotArmController with an ArmBotFF instance and retrieves telemetry.

MRILib.managers.PivotArmController#run().description: Executes the main control loop for the pivot arm. It continuously updates the arm's state, calculates feedforward and feedback control signals, determines the appropriate motor power based on the current pivot mode, and applies the calculated power to the motor. The loop runs at a fixed interval, ensuring consistent control.

MRILib.managers.PivotArmController#setMo.description: Applies the calculated power to the pivot motor, adjusting it based on the robot's current voltage to maintain consistent performance. The power is clipped to a safe range (-0.9 to 1.0).

MRILib.managers.PivotArmController#calcu.description: Calculates the feedforward compensation for gravitational forces acting on the arm. It uses pre-defined constants and the arm's current position and mass distribution to estimate the required force to counteract gravity.

MRILib.managers.PivotArmController#updat.description: Updates the internal state of the pivot arm, including its angular position, angular velocity, and slide position. It also recalculates the arm's center of mass and current arm theta based on the new inputs. Returns the current arm theta in degrees.
Updates the internal state of the pivot arm with arm position and velocity, defaulting the slide position to 0.

MRILib.managers.PivotArmController#setPi.description: Sets the operational mode of the pivot arm controller.

MRILib.managers.PivotArmController#setIn.description: Sets the input power for the RUN_WITH_POWER mode.

MRILib.managers.PivotArmController#setTa.description: Sets the target angular position for the pivot arm, accepting the target angle in either radians or degrees.
Sets the target angular position for the pivot arm, defaulting the input angle to degrees.

MRILib.managers.PivotArmController#setTh.description: Sets an offset to be applied to the arm's current angle readings, typically used for calibration.

MRILib.managers.PivotArmController#stop(.description: Stops the execution of the pivot arm controller's main loop.

MRILib.motion.HeadingPID#HeadingPID(doub.description: Constructs a new HeadingPID controller, initializing the proportional (kP), integral (kI), and derivative (kD) gains by calling the superclass PID constructor.

MRILib.motion.HeadingPID#update(double).description: Updates the PID controller for angular values. It calculates the shortest angular error using Mathf.angleWrap, applies integral anti-windup by limiting error summation, computes the derivative of the wrapped error, and returns the total control output. It also handles potential division by zero for deltaTime and updates internal state (lastTime, lastError) for the next iteration.

MRILib.motion.PID#PID(double,double,doub.description: Constructs a PID controller instance with specified proportional (p), integral (i), and derivative (d) gains. Initializes the internal timer and starts it to ensure accurate delta time calculation from the first update.

MRILib.motion.PID#start().description: Resets the internal state of the PID controller. Clears the accumulated integral error, the last calculated error, and the last timestamp. Resets the internal timer to zero, preparing the controller for a new control sequence.

MRILib.motion.PID#update(double).description: Calculates the PID control output based on the current measured value. It computes the error, delta time, integral term (with anti-windup clamping), and derivative term. Updates internal state (last error, last time) for the next iteration. Returns the combined P, I, and D control output.

MRILib.motion.PID#update(double,double).description: Selects the input value (current1 or current2) that is closer to the target value and then calls the primary `update(double current)` method with the selected value. Returns the calculated control output.

MRILib.motion.PID#setTarget(double).description: Sets the target value (setpoint) for the PID controller. The controller will now aim to minimize the error between this new target and the measured values.

MRILib.motion.PID#setILimit(double).description: Sets the integral limit (iLimit). The integral term will only accumulate when the absolute error is less than this limit, helping to prevent integral windup. Ensures the provided limit is non-negative.

MRILib.motion.PID#setIntegralSumLimit(do.description: Sets the maximum absolute value for the accumulated integral error sum (errorSumTotal). This clamps the integral term to prevent excessive buildup, further aiding in anti-windup. Ensures the provided limit is non-negative.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#PIDControlle.description: Constructs a PIDController, initializing the bot and telemetry interfaces, and setting default maximum speed suppliers.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#setPID(PID,P.description: Assigns PID controllers for the X and Y axes of movement.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#setTurnPID(P.description: Assigns the PID controller for rotational (theta) movement. Recommends using HeadingPID for angle wrapping.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#start().description: Initializes the PID controllers by resetting their internal states and setting the initial target for the theta PID based on the last `moveTo` call.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#update().description: Updates the robot's movement based on PID controller outputs. Reads current pose, calculates PID corrections for X, Y, and theta, applies dynamic speed limits, and commands the robot to drive using field-centric coordinates via `bot.driveFieldXYW`.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#moveTo(doubl.description: Sets the target position (X, Y coordinates) and heading (degrees) for the robot. Updates the targets for the X, Y, and Theta PID controllers.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#setMaxSpeed(.description: Sets a constant maximum translational speed limit for the robot, clipped between 0.0 and 1.0.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#setMaxAngSpe.description: Sets a constant maximum angular (rotational) speed limit for the robot, clipped between 0.0 and 1.0.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#getTargetX().description: Retrieves the currently set target X-coordinate in inches.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#getTargetY().description: Retrieves the currently set target Y-coordinate in inches.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#getTargetRad.description: Retrieves the currently set target heading in radians.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#getTargetDeg.description: Retrieves the currently set target heading in degrees by converting the internal radian value.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#getCurrentDe.description: Retrieves the robot's current heading in degrees from the odometry system, potentially applying a negation based on convention.

MRILib.motion.PIDController#getCurrentRa.description: Retrieves the robot's current heading in radians from the odometry system, potentially applying a negation based on convention.
